headline: GM, Jeep Parent Stellantis Say No Disruptions After Ohio Glass Factory Fire
summary: Investigators are probing the cause of a fire that burned a section of a large automotive glass plant near Dayton, Ohio, that supplies components to several major automakers.  The roof of the Fuyao Glass America plant, said to be one of the largest automotive-glass manufacturing plants in the world, caught fire around 8:30 p.m. Sunday, according to city officials in Moraine, Ohio.  City officials said no factory employees or fire crews were injured.

City officials said no factory employees or fire crews were injured.

headline: Auto demand seems strained. Could EVs make a comeback?
summary: Cox Automotive anticipates slower 2026 auto sales due to pressures from gas (RB=F) prices and interest rates.Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian outlines the details, highlighting how the macroeconomic landscape benefits the electric vehicles (EVs) and hybrids.

headline: Hybrids now account for 1 in 5 U.S. vehicle sales as market shifts
summary: “Hybrids now are almost one out of every five vehicles that were sold in the United States in the fourth quarter.” That single data point reshapes how you should think about the U.S. auto market right now and which companies are positioned to benefit from it. The shift has been rapid. Hybrid market share surged ... Hybrids now account for 1 in 5 U.S. vehicle sales as market shifts

headline: GM upgraded at Wolfe Research as firm sees ‘select opportunities’ in autos
summary: Investing.com -- Wolfe Research has upgraded General Motors to Outperform, arguing in a note Wednesday that the recent market sell-off has created attractive entry points across parts of the autos sector.

headline: Ford vs. Stellantis: Which Automaker Stock Has the Edge Now?
summary: F stands out over STLA with steady revenue growth, strong Ford Pro momentum, and a solid dividend, while STLA faces rising debt and no payout.

headline: Ford Has Run Out Of Places To Go
summary: There are many regions worldwide where cars can be sold. However, without success in China, the EU, and the US, global automotive companies get painted into a corner. Ford’s (NYSE: F) US sales often rank third behind (NYSE: GM) and Toyota (NYSE: TM). Ford makes money in China, but exports are critical to its profit ... Ford Has Run Out Of Places To Go

headline: Assessing Ford Motor (F) Valuation After New MLB Partnership And EV Sustainability Updates
summary: Ford Motor (F) has given investors a fresh storyline by becoming Major League Baseball’s new official automotive partner after two decades of Chevrolet, while also highlighting recent moves in sustainability and electric vehicle collaborations. See our latest analysis for Ford Motor. Ford’s recent MLB partnership, sustainability work at its new Marshall facility and EV grid projects sit against a mixed share price picture, with a 1-month share price return decline of 15.49% but a 1-year total...

headline: The Bull Case For Ford Motor (F) Could Change Following New MLB Deal And V2H Energy Push
summary: In recent weeks, Puget Sound Energy and ChargeScape have begun testing vehicle-to-home technology with partners including Ford, while Ford has also announced an exclusive multiyear partnership with Major League Baseball and a new construction waste-reduction initiative at its Marshall, Michigan facility. Together, these moves highlight Ford’s push to pair brand reach in American sports with practical energy-resilience and sustainability solutions tied directly to its vehicles and...
